patterns_matched: - **Before**: `from twilio.twiml.voice_response import VoiceResponse; r = VoiceResponse(); r.dial(...); return HttpResponse(r)`
This app shows you how to implement a sample call tracking application with Twilio. Add a new phone number below to get started.

config_files.line: - **Before**: `from twilio.twiml.voice_response import VoiceResponse; r = VoiceResponse(); r.dial(...); return HttpResponse(r)`

Use Twilio to track the effectiveness of your different marketing campaigns. Learn how call tracking helps organizations in [these Twilio customer stories](https://www.twilio.com/use-cases/call-tracking).

Create a new TwiML app [in the Twilio Console](https://console.twilio.com/us1/develop/voice/manage/twiml-apps?frameUrl=%2Fconsole%2Fvoice%2Ftwiml%2Fapps%3Fx-target-region%3Dus1) and use its `Sid` as the `TWIML_APPLICATION_SID` environment variable wherever you run this app.
| Account Sid  | Your primary Twilio account identifier - find this [in the Console](https://www.twilio.com/console).                                                         |
| Auth Token   | Used to authenticate - [just like the above, you'll find this here](https://www.twilio.com/console).                                                         |

| TwiML app Sid | TwiML app SID can be found by clicking on your [TwiML App in the console](https://console.twilio.com/us1/develop/voice/manage/twiml-apps?frameUrl=%2Fconsole%2Fvoice%2Ftwiml%2Fapps%3Fx-target-region%3Dus1) |
1. Copy the `.env.example` file to `.env`, and edit it to include your Twilio API credentials (found at https://www.twilio.com/user/account/voice)

To actually forward incoming calls, your development server will need to be publicly accessible. [We recommend using ngrok to solve this problem](https://www.twilio.com/blog/2013/10/test-your-webhooks-locally-with-ngrok.html).
1. Copy the `.env.prod.example` file to `.env`, and edit it to include your Twilio API credentials (found at https://www.twilio.com/user/account/voice)
- **Before**: `from twilio.twiml.voice_response import VoiceResponse; r = VoiceResponse(); r.dial(...); return HttpResponse(r)`

# Telnyx API configuration - migrated from Twilio
1. `call_tracking/utils.py` - Replaced Twilio SDK with Telnyx SDK
- ✅ All Twilio webhook validation patterns removed
4. **Port Numbers** (optional): If keeping existing phone numbers, use FastPort to port from Twilio
"pattern": "# Telnyx API configuration - migrated from Twilio",
"# Telnyx API configuration - migrated from Twilio",
"pattern": "\"\"\"Uses the Twilio API to generate a list of available phone numbers\"\"\"",
"    \"\"\"Uses the Twilio API to generate a list of available phone numbers\"\"\"",
"pattern": "# We received a valid area code - query the Twilio API",
"        # We received a valid area code - query the Twilio API",
"pattern": "'There are no Twilio numbers available for area code {0}. Search for numbers in a different area code.'.format(area_code))",
"                'There are no Twilio numbers available for area code {0}. Search for numbers in a different area code.'.format(area_code))",
"pattern": "\"\"\"Purchases a new phone number using the Twilio API\"\"\"",
"    \"\"\"Purchases a new phone number using the Twilio API\"\"\"",
"pattern": "# Check if it's Telnyx (JSON) or legacy Twilio (form data)",
"    # Check if it's Telnyx (JSON) or legacy Twilio (form data)",
"pattern": "# Raw XML string (Telnyx doesn't have a builder class like Twilio)",
"    # Raw XML string (Telnyx doesn't have a builder class like Twilio)",
"        help_text='A phone number purchased through Twilio')",
"pattern": "# URLs for searching for and purchasing a new Twilio number",
"    # URLs for searching for and purchasing a new Twilio number",
"pattern": "# Endpoint Twilio will use for incoming calls",
"    # Endpoint Twilio will use for incoming calls",
"pattern": "\"\"\"Form for purchasing a number from the Twilio API\"\"\"",
"    \"\"\"Form for purchasing a number from the Twilio API\"\"\"",
"pattern": "('incoming_number', phonenumber_field.modelfields.PhoneNumberField(help_text='A phone number purchased through Twilio', max_length=128, unique=True)),",
"                ('incoming_number', phonenumber_field.modelfields.PhoneNumberField(help_text='A phone number purchased through Twilio', max_length=128, unique=True)),",
"""Uses the Twilio API to generate a list of available phone numbers"""
# We received a valid area code - query the Twilio API
'There are no Twilio numbers available for area code {0}. Search for numbers in a different area code.'.format(area_code))
"""Purchases a new phone number using the Twilio API"""
# Check if it's Telnyx (JSON) or legacy Twilio (form data)
# Raw XML string (Telnyx doesn't have a builder class like Twilio)
help_text='A phone number purchased through Twilio')
# URLs for searching for and purchasing a new Twilio number
# Endpoint Twilio will use for incoming calls
('incoming_number', phonenumber_field.modelfields.PhoneNumberField(help_text='A phone number purchased through Twilio', max_length=128, unique=True)),
"""Form for purchasing a number from the Twilio API"""

Use Twilio to track the effectiveness of your different marketing campaigns. Learn how call tracking helps organizations in [these Twilio customer stories](https://www.twilio.com/use-cases/call-tracking).
This project is configured to use a **TwiML App**, which allows us to easily set the voice URLs for all Twilio phone numbers we purchase in this app.

Create a new TwiML app [in the Twilio Console](https://console.twilio.com/us1/develop/voice/manage/twiml-apps?frameUrl=%2Fconsole%2Fvoice%2Ftwiml%2Fapps%3Fx-target-region%3Dus1) and use its `Sid` as the `TWIML_APPLICATION_SID` environment variable wherever you run this app.
| Account Sid  | Your primary Twilio account identifier - find this [in the Console](https://www.twilio.com/console).                                                         |
1. Copy the `.env.example` file to `.env`, and edit it to include your Twilio API credentials (found at https://www.twilio.com/user/account/voice)
1. Copy the `.env.prod.example` file to `.env`, and edit it to include your Twilio API credentials (found at https://www.twilio.com/user/account/voice)
* Lovingly crafted by Twilio Developer Education.
